name: ilspy-vscode
description: Decompile MSIL assemblies - support for full framework, .NET Core and .NET Standard

ilspy.defaultOutputLanguage.description: Defines to which language or language variant ILSpy will decompile assemblies by default. Note that you can choose a different language for a specific view later.
enumDescriptions: Decompile to readable IL instructions by default

ilspy.loadPreviousAssemblies.description: If activated, the list of loaded assemblies is stored with current workspace and is restored on re-opening. This doesn't apply for temporarily auto-loaded dependencies.

ilspy.autoLoadDependencies.description: If activated, dependency assemblies are temporarily added to assembly tree on demand.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No assemblies selected yet
[Add assembly from workspace](command:ilspy.decompileAssemblyInWorkspace)
[Pick assembly from file system](command:ilspy.decompileAssemblyViaDialog)

Take the [Feature Tour](https://github.com/icsharpcode/ilspy-vscode/wiki/Feature-Tour)

commands.title: Add assembly or package from workspace
Pick assembly or package from file system

view/item/context.when: (view == ilspyDecompiledMembers || view == ilspySearchResultsContainer || view == ilspyAnalyzeResultsContainer) && viewItem =~ /#analyze/
(view == ilspyDecompiledMembers || view == ilspySearchResultsContainer || view == ilspyAnalyzeResultsContainer) && viewItem =~ /#analyze/

languageModelTools.name: add_loaded_assembly
languageModelTools.displayName: Add assembly or NuGet package to ILSpy
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Adds one assembly or NuGet package file to the set of assemblies currently loaded in ILSpy so that it can be searched, analyzed, decompiled, and exported.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Add one assembly or NuGet package file to ILSpy

assemblyPath.description: Absolute file system path of the assembly to add to ILSpy

languageModelTools.name: remove_loaded_assembly
languageModelTools.displayName: Remove assembly or NuGet package from ILSpy
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Removes one currently loaded assembly or NuGet package from ILSpy. Prefer assemblyPath for exact targeting; assemblyName can be used when it uniquely identifies a loaded assembly.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Remove one loaded assembly or NuGet package from ILSpy

assemblyPath.description: Absolute file system path of the loaded assembly or NuGet package to remove

assemblyName.description: Assembly file name or simple assembly name to remove when it is unique among loaded assemblies

languageModelTools.name: list_loaded_assemblies
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the assemblies or NuGet packages currently loaded in ILSpy, including their names and full paths. Use this before decompilation or analysis when you need to know what ILSpy can already inspect.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List the assemblies or NuGet packages currently loaded in ILSpy

languageModelTools.name: decompile_symbol
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches the currently loaded ILSpy assemblies for a .NET symbol such as a type, member, or namespace, then returns the decompiled source code for the matching symbol or symbols. Use assemblyPath or assemblyName to disambiguate when the same symbol exists in multiple assemblies.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Decompile a .NET symbol from the assemblies currently loaded in ILSpy

symbol.description: Name of the .NET symbol to decompile. A fully qualified name is preferred when available

assemblyPath.description: Optional absolute assembly path used to narrow the symbol search to one loaded assembly

assemblyName.description: Optional assembly file name or simple assembly name used to narrow the symbol search

outputLanguage.description: Optional ILSpy output language. When omitted, the extension's current default output language is used

languageModelTools.name: decompile_node
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Decompiles one specific ILSpy tree node using its exact nodeMetadata payload. Use this when you already have a concrete node from list_nodes or another ILSpy tool result, especially for non-symbol nodes such as references, resources, or other tree entries that are not best addressed by symbol search.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Decompile one specific ILSpy tree node

nodeMetadata.description: Exact ILSpy node metadata, typically copied verbatim from the nodeMetadata field returned by list_nodes or another ILSpy tool result

assemblyPath.description: Assembly path associated with the node

type.description: Numeric ILSpy node type enum value

bundledAssemblyName.description: Optional bundled assembly name for package-backed nodes

symbolToken.description: Node symbol token from ILSpy metadata

parentSymbolToken.description: Parent symbol token from ILSpy metadata

availableCommands.description: Bitmask of commands ILSpy reports as available for the node

outputLanguage.description: Optional ILSpy output language. When omitted, the extension's current default output language is used

languageModelTools.name: list_nodes
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists nodes from ILSpy's assembly tree. With no input it returns the root nodes, which are usually the loaded assemblies. Prefer passing exact nodeMetadata from a prior list_nodes result to navigate to the next level of any tree node. Symbol or assembly inputs are still supported as shortcuts for choosing the first target. Results include exact nodeMetadata for further tree navigation or decompilation.

nodeMetadata.description: Exact ILSpy node metadata, typically copied verbatim from the nodeMetadata field returned by list_nodes or another ILSpy tool result. Prefer this for navigating deeper into the tree from any node type.

assemblyPath.description: Assembly path associated with the node

type.description: Numeric ILSpy node type enum value

bundledAssemblyName.description: Optional bundled assembly name for package-backed nodes

symbolToken.description: Node symbol token from ILSpy metadata

parentSymbolToken.description: Parent symbol token from ILSpy metadata

availableCommands.description: Bitmask of commands ILSpy reports as available for the node

symbol.description: Optional symbol whose direct children should be listed when nodeMetadata is not provided

assemblyPath.description: Optional absolute assembly path whose direct child nodes should be listed, or used to disambiguate a symbol when nodeMetadata is not provided

assemblyName.description: Optional assembly file name or simple assembly name whose direct child nodes should be listed, or used to disambiguate a symbol when nodeMetadata is not provided

languageModelTools.name: search_decompiled_symbols
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches the symbols in assemblies currently loaded in ILSpy and returns matching nodes with type, assembly path, display information, and exact nodeMetadata. Use it to discover exact symbol names before decompiling or analyzing them, or to obtain nodeMetadata for decompile_node.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search the symbols in assemblies currently loaded in ILSpy

term.description: Text to search for in loaded assemblies

assemblyPath.description: Optional absolute assembly path used to restrict the search to one loaded assembly

assemblyName.description: Optional assembly file name or simple assembly name used to restrict the search

languageModelTools.name: analyze_symbol
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs ILSpy's analyze request for a loaded symbol or assembly and returns the resulting analyzer nodes. Use it to inspect usages, derived types, base types, or other analysis views exposed by ILSpy.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze a loaded symbol or assembly with ILSpy

symbol.description: Optional symbol to analyze. If omitted, assemblyPath or assemblyName must identify a loaded assembly

assemblyPath.description: Optional absolute assembly path used to target or disambiguate the analysis request

assemblyName.description: Optional assembly file name or simple assembly name used to target or disambiguate the analysis request

languageModelTools.name: export_decompiled_code
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Exports the decompiled output of a loaded ILSpy node to a directory on disk. The target can be an assembly or another exportable symbol. Use assemblyPath or assemblyName to target a loaded assembly directly, or provide a symbol and optionally narrow it by assembly.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Export decompiled ILSpy output to a directory on disk

symbol.description: Optional export target symbol. If omitted, assemblyPath or assemblyName must identify a loaded assembly

assemblyPath.description: Optional absolute assembly path used to target or disambiguate the export

assemblyName.description: Optional assembly file name or simple assembly name used to target or disambiguate the export

outputDirectory.description: Absolute path of the directory where ILSpy should write the exported files

outputLanguage.description: Optional ILSpy output language. When omitted, the extension's current default output language is used

includeCompilerGenerated.description: Optional override for whether compiler-generated members should be included in the export
